channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /Biz revival — 105 replies — 420.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 105 • Updated: 2026-05-25 13:46 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62266502
>>62266484
no

from western europe btw. Not telling you were it's a faggy country
>>62266493
post it. do u post on /pol too? ibet you goon to hentai, never visited any other board than pol or biz

62266521
>>62266502
No I actually was the anon who got people to turn on tranime posting here lol, I never got into anime besides Berserk but that feels like gospel at this point everyone knows that.
I don't have it saved lol, I came from /pol/ after being a lolbert activist in the 2010s and stumbled my way almost to 100k in 4 months of being on /biz/ from being broke.
Then lost most of it, then met multiple millionaires, and then talked to Mark Cuban during the bullrun it was a wild experience.

I don't really post on /pol/ unless Israel is being bombed.

62266580
>>62266521
yes pol is too cringe and brown nowadays, i still go there to hurl racial insults from time to time and uplift my mood.
Biz is also very bad with 0 financial value but occasionnaly you make a valuable discovery. it's where i discovered LITS (yeah i know you don't like litecoin)
The reality is that it takes a lot of digging to find gems.

62266591
>>62266580
I'm just giving you shit, I've made money off litecoin multiple times.
and still the cheapest to transfer money around like for example you need to move 10k from coinbase to kraken, using BTC or ETH is gonna cost you an arm and a leg.
LTC is remarkably cheaper.

62266600
>>62266580
Last attempt I had was 10k on a coin that just released all Althea on Kraken, absolutely got buttfucked bought at 4 dollars when it released 6 dollars, looked like a solid project and was early............
...
Dropped to 4 cents think it's still there, but probably a good investment now, not when I bought.

62266609
An anon tries to create a decent thread and it gets instantly sabotaged.

62266618
>>62266600
>>62266591
that's why i buy litecoin. I don't want to buy something that will go to 0, i'm not looking for a x100 which is too hard to find.
Rn at 50$ ltc, has very little downside and a lot of upside. Even if it does a 3x at best, that's a relatively safe and ez 3x. Of course it could go lower even 0 everything is possible but highly unlikely.
And it could moon in the future because everyone uses it and somehow it hasn't shined yet

62266643
>>62265467
Akash, DePin actually seems to be getting some traction

62267090
>>62266618
it would have to get hacked or somehow so toxic everyone delists it even then only way its going to 0 is if btc goes to 0

62267267
>>62267090
i'll look into it but it's already 2x / 3x from bottom

62267325
>>62267267
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q4vF3o4Ss6g

62267354
>>62265467
Lillybux is the next /biz/coin

62267369
>>62265467
greetings anon i have just returned from a hiatus therefore the board is alive again. the only coin you need now is $PUMP. the irony is $PUMP killed crypto by making it possible to launch so many coins that there became no community like there used to be, so now we have to invert that into wealth by buying PUMP. you'll see. bull run hasn't even begun.

62267402
Every single factor that led to GME and CAR going parabolic is perfectly aligned for GRPN in 6 days.

62267687
>>62265467
I regret selling my litecoin and I've bought some of it back. It has a strong narrative as OG coin. There's the livVM being worked on which will bring eth style smart contracts to it. IDK how decentralized it will be but it's a start.

ICP. Do yourself a favor and actually read some of the tech details of how it works. They're setting up "sovereign networks" signing deals with heads of state. Prepping for the future. Chainkey cryptography is wizardry.
The biggest downside, IMO, is the UI is pretty shit - you have to sign a lot of "canisters" (smart contracts) to get going. But it's the only tech that combines blockchain with website plus is reasonably distributed.

62269707
>>62267687
litecoin has developped MWEB network, which is basically private transactions integrated into the already ltc blockchain. it's a pretty big deal, like an integrated coin mixer that is fully private (can't decipher it just like monero).
Very few apps use it yet.
Once it finally gets some sufficient support on any ltc wallet, it will be much like zcash. You go into MWEB, and every public trace stop there. you can also go out of mweb of course.

62269750
Is everyone here stuck in 2017? How about a coin that has come out in the past half decade gramps.

62270867
Ntdoy and rare earth etfs. Some uranium too (nuclear nrgy not nukes)

62270942
I find it so hard to browse this board these days, the humor is so so tiring. Just the same jokes over and over again. I know some people say they find gems here every know and then but I never have, anyone got any advice? I’m so tired man

62271126
>>62265467
Why buy LITS instead of LTC?

62271298
>>62271126
because it's like buying litecoin with a discount, and when ltc pumps LITS will pump multiple X more just like micro strategy did with btc

62271371
>>62265467
>Ton
Turns out that's in my bookmarks as something with potential. I looked at Litecoin when I first got into crypto, but I figured these long-standing projects like it, LINK, HBAR, etc. were things I'd come back to later with gains from lower market cap stuff. Seems that was the correct decision since so many of them are now around the same price as they day I bought my first assets.
I figured they might make great strides someday but not in a hurry since they'd already make great strides (price-wise) since their debuts. Despite that I'm shocked to see Litecoin is like half the price of when I first looked at it.
I'd recommend some stuff myself but I'm still in that newish area where I've made good picks after reading up on stuff before buying (the DYOR part /biz/ always told me to do) but couldn't briefly articulate why someone should take a look at something at current prices. Everything that's done me well is more expensive now than when I first bought it.

62271377
>>62265877
I don't think there's a LUNC thread up now but I remember seeing one recently where there was a lot of reasonable optimism.

62271378
>>62265467
It's dead Jim

62271387
>>62270867
What's the basis for uranium at this time? I saw a few months ago people talking about various reactors, from new mini ones to restarting some existing ones.

62271407
>>62271387
an anon shilled some us stock, nano nuclear energy and i didn't buy because the chart looked already pumped. it went down a lot since like 40%

Nano nuclear energy. I looked it up they're trying to develop mini nuclear reactors thant can be moved around, like for data centers etc. But i'm wondering isn't that tech super risky?in any case that might or might not be a great moonshot. No idea, don't own any but looking at it

62271421
>>62271407
I can see what you would be. I remember seeing some airplane-lifted reactors some months ago. Operation Windlord. Valar Atomics, though, they are not publicly traded.
I see what you mean about NNE having doing about a 10x since it began and now being like 50% down since then. Still, nice to have specific leads on what to research.

62271501
>>62265467
Its dead because there havent been enough nigger days due to the bull market

62271653
How about starting with removing this faggot nigger captcha and having to wait for a minute for it? I gave up posting here, because of it, honestly. And I'm not verifying my email each time either.

62271675
>>62271653
Captchas become faster and easier if you post more

item.title: HODL and buy more BTC. — 94 replies — 376.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HODL and buy more BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-05-25 13:52 UTC

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757
How much BTC are you holding right now anon?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261452
>>62260090
100k by October

62261462
>>62261452
no chance
this will count as the summer of the big dump

62261476
>>62261462
Your opinion doesn't matter to me

62261608
>>62261340
Why do people still not know about this?
Better to explore and understand the TBV direction early before launch.

62261618
>>62261462
How do you know?

62261632
>>62252488
Poor mindset

62261641
>>62261131
Fee: $20000

62262278
>>62259397
Buy altcoins too

62262820
>>62261608
No need to rush. People will figure it out eventually. You can already see more attention shifting toward DeFi outside the usual Ethereum-only lane anyway.

62262829
>>62261131
And people still wonder why institutions keep accumulating BTC.

62262834
>>62256924
Depends on their choice tho

62262852
Bitcoin is the future

62262893
>>62262278
>Hbar
>Link
>Rave
>Sei

62264806
>>62262852
And the present

62265221
>>62258793
Devil

62265362
Buy 10btc

62267557
>>62262893
Not bad

62267594
>>62250313
I'd be happy for $500 kek

62268019
I am aiming for having 0,1 BTC by the end of the year (got a nasty 3'000$ tax bill in august and september) but aside from that I'm going all in every month from now.

62268405
>>62260090
with what money

62268430
>>62253499
Checked. I appreciate the assessment.

62268528
>>62268430
When moon?

62269040
>>62268019
going all in is not recommended idiot

62269062
>>62269040
I can handle it.

62269119
>>62269040
if you have very little moeny, going all in is your only shot. You either make it or you stay poor a little longer, status quo.

62269122
>>62269119
*money

62269256
>>62269062
Then good luck

62269965
>>62246438
Less than 0.1 kek, but I'm buying more. I'm thinking of staking too, I noticed some BTCFI now gives a chance to stake without wrap or bridge.

62270744
>>62269965
Kek

62271690
>>62269062
>>62269119
You can do it anon

item.title: Why is US consumer sentiment so low — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-25 13:47 UTC

OP

Why is US consumer sentiment so low

Early replies (first 3)

62269461
>>62269455
>be burger
>vote retard
>retard do trade war and real war
>cost living up wtf

62269462
>>62269455
they have no money?

62269465
>>62269461
basically this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269478
>>62269461
cry more libtard

62269489
everyone is drinking bearish propaganda

62269502
>>62269455
Sales at my company are down 95% from last year

62269508
A fucking snickers bar costs like $3

I used to buy them with pocket change as a kid, everything sucks, shit quality, and is expensive as fuck.

62269526
Damn, who would have thought expanding the money supply by 40% during covid would demolish wagies purchasing power by 40%

62269530
Bro just use a.i. and you're good
Stop being a doomer

62269542
America used to export inflation to other countries and now dollar is falling against every currency in the world. What happened?

62269549
>>62269455
Shut

Up

62269560
>>62269455
They want to do more than just look at porn for some reason

62269610
>>62269455
Half of Americans are finding themselves on the wrong side of the K shaped economy.

>>62269526
The greatest demonstration of why basic income would never work. Give everyone $1000 a month and everything just goes up in price to compensate.

62269614
>>62269461
>vote retard
What else was on the ballot?

62269636
>>62269614
A BLUE RETARD

62269639
it's just that time of month. i feel most business startups are just entertainment for the proles. the businesses that do matter and make an impact on advancing research costs a lot and is bound to recieve government attention. not everyone can be a rocket scientist. maybe a lot can, but only if they had all the resources and time, but as we know we are limited pretending not to. statistics don't lie that we're in a pinch. why not just become slaves again? why not concentrate all the resources? yes there is a lot of potential for the young, but why prioritize them when you have people that already know what they are doing? the young should realize this, for if not knowing how to struggle, the more resources they will drain anyways. i don't mean this to be ragebait btw, but when times are tough people tend to move to extremes and things get scary

62269641
>>62269610
>the greatest demonstration of why basic income would never work. Give everyone $1000 a month and everything just goes up in price to compensate.
the point is to fundamentally change how incentives are doled out. instead of giving massive subsidies to corporations that in turn waste money on AI and jack up prices on everything, the destitute formerly employed wagies are given money to buy food and pay rent.

62269643
Miracle economy

62269647
>>62269461
Jews control every voteable optio

62269718
>>62269455
AI taking all our jerbs

62269725
I went to canes, a notriously overpriced and overrated chicken place today at noon.

The drive through was packed and when I say packed there was 40+ cars in line. There were so many people that there was a line into the parking lot and then out of it into the other parking lot across the restaurant, with the cars having to make a space for traffic to pass.

On top of that they parking lot for canes was full and the restaurant was full.


So if people are so doom and gloom poor, complaining that everything is expensive why was there 70-80 people eating lunch at an overpriced chicken joint at max capacity

62269728
>>62269455
ONLY REAL ANSWER IN THE WHOLE FUCKING THREAD: the methodology changed, the survey is now mostly democrat, instead of being 50/50 democrat/republican. Actually the sentiment of either democrats and republicans has not changed for some time now... however if the % of democrats slowly goes up in the survey then you will see the consumer sentiment value drop over time.

62269747
>>62269461
>>vote retard
RETARDS ARE THE ONLY OPTIONS WE'RE EVER GIVEN

62269798
>>62269461
MIGA
I
G
A

62269874
>>62269455
Because people are cooked.
Something broke after covid, a lot of people stopped living in the real world and became citizens of the internet instead.
The economy is doing okay, maybe even good, but the doom and gloom that is pervasive on the internet, has led a lot of people to believe otherwise.

62269888
>>62269455
Job market. Who wants to make big purchase when it's so hard find a new job.

62269909
>>62269725
It's not that overpriced $14 for 3(4?) tenders and coleslaw, fries n toast plus drink? That's pretty much any fast food place anymore. The big arch meal was $17>
People are depressed and regular food is expensive as hell too. It used to be buying food at the grocery store was so much cheaper you can't justify it, but now it is only so much cheaper and tastes worse if you suck at cooking, many do.
However the amount of "I'm only eating beans" posting I see makes me think that is also happening.

62269918
>>62269874
Wew lawd to be able to cope this hard, may I have a puff of that?

62270130
>>62269455
because that retarded orange nigger decided to double the debt, rape consumer pockets with his tariffs, then gift them to corpofaggots. Fuck that dumbass, braindead, dipshit, rapist, incompetent, pissant little nigger. Fuck you too cunt.

62270242
>>62269874
fucking doom and gloomers

https://www.tiktok.com/@hog_tok1/video/7308692257761234222

62270350
>>62269455
Because it's time for the crisis

62271678
>>62269614
amerimutts shit themselves and decided to pick the faggot who made everything worse for them instead of voting for the brown bitch kek

The important thing is that sergay got unimaginably wealthy from Link and our sacrifice to him. I, like all of my fellow Link Marines, live to serve our master Sergay. Whatever I can do to satisfy Sergay, financially, sexually, or otherwise, I will do so with a smile. I am Sergay’s.

fuddie gooner sissies can't understand how satisfying it is to see Sergey getting rich!

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 27 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-25 10:57 UTC

OP

KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE

Early replies (first 3)

62269301
>>62269297
Oh thank God!

62269302
Won’t do shit
Trump won’t allow it

62269305
Can this nigga wait until btc dumps to $30k plsss

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269309
>Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's unconventional strategy aims to shrink the Fed's bloated balance sheet—including selling off its roughly \(\$2\) trillion in mortgage-backed securities—while cutting short-term interest rates. By removing the Fed from the market, he intends to pop pandemic-era asset bubbles, lower broader mortgage rates, and return housing affordability to historical norms

62269311
20k October

62269319
>>62269309
Deporting 100 million would also solve housing.

62269321
>>62269297
imagine taking out a 100k 5x leveraged investment and it drops 20k in value while index funds go up like 40%

62269323
>>62269319
Anything but solving the real issue. Kick the can down the road enough and you'll go too far to turn back before you know it.

62269325
>>62269319
They've started going after financial institutions who service illegals and will probably start going after other institutions too in order to force illegals to self-deport. So it appears that they're doing both

62269418
>>62269297
So?

62269424
>>62269309
>KEVIN WARSH: WE ARE GOING TO POP THE HOUSING MARKET BUBBLE
Lowing rates increases the price of housing, trump with his real estate assets said he won't let pricing come down.

62269437

62269442
>>62269319

62269443

62269468

62269473

62269536

62269553

62269564

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 259 replies — 54.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 259 • Updated: 2026-05-25 09:08 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265888
>>62259935
You know you can easily have biological children without marriage right?

Just fucking cum in some young woman and keep doing it until ahe misses a period. If that makes you fags squeamish just pay some surrogate to carry your child while you enjoy escorts or casual dating and sex.

It really isn't that hard, and after the surrogate pushes your child out it'll be the last time you need to hear a woman yell at you ever again lol.

62265896
>>62264378
>This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.
the stemfag market was goated 12 years ago, now you have to be the throte goat to find anything

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

62269694
>>62268709
It's pretty easy if you let her use a wand or clit sucking vibrator to give herself orgasms. The only tricky part is YOU cumming during one of her 5 or 8 orgasms.

62269751
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing
Why not? Are you a woman? It's usually only parasites annoyed by this.

62269890
>incel bait (((mysteriously))) ruins another anti waging thread
this reddit poster is obviously some low test gooner soiboi. In reality 1.9m in an index fund can get you around 150k a year which is more than enough for a non-incel life especially with a paid off house.

62269901
>>62259935
I'm 37 and once I hit a 2m net worth in the next couple of years I'm going to "retire" and go back into academia. It doesn't pay very well but at least the work is enjoyable and I get to spend time with other scientists instead of these bullshit artists in industry.

62269979
>>62259935
No women? The entire point of FIRE is to fuck wives while the husband works...did you even read the manifesto?

62270348
>>62259935
I'm 40 with only ~$400k net worth. No kids/wife/never been on a date. $25k annual spend.
Just get me a rope...

62270366
How dare you??
You are just a damn child.

62270374
>>62270348
I had $400k net worth when I was 40. I'm 44 now with $1.7 million net worth. A lot can change in a short amount of time.

62270394
>>62270374
>A lot can change in a short amount of time.
Bitcoin's growth is slower now and that's my only hope.
My salary is currently $100k and I don't see potential for any significant raises in my future.
At least I think I'll have the balls to exit the system at $1M and maybe find a part time job stocking shelves to keep me busy.

62270406
>>62270348
You're not missing anything. Society is fucked. I didn't start dating until I was ~33. One woman that I dated when I was 34 ruined me forever. Not financially - just mentally.

I'm worse off financially than you because of student loans but in a few more years, I'll be in better shape albeit still with like half of your net worth when I hit your age. You're in okay shape. You're doing much better than most of your peers. You can get the validation from a woman if you push for it but it won't help. I got validation from other women after the crazy one and I didn't feel a thing. Hopefully you have nieces and nephews for whom you can leave something behind. That's my plan even though I'll probably never even meet my nieces or nephews. So it goes.

62270414
>>62270406
Thanks, bro. I've got my music, I guess.
>nieces and nephews
Nah. I'm a single child raised by a single mother. Once she's gone, I'm all alone.

62270422
>>62270414
Sorry man. My nieces and nephews are the children of my cousins, not any siblings, and I don't have a relationship with my cousins. For all intents and purposes, I'll be pretty alone when my parents are gone and they're both up there in age.

Music is my thing too. I don't enjoy it as much anymore though. Nothing hits the same after that woman. You still have a lot of time to find someone especially since you have your shit together financially but just be careful. I don't want anyone to ever have to feel the way I do on account of a woman.

62270444
>>62270422
I hope things will work out for us in the end.
I'd definitely rather be in a band (something like Jaga Jazzist) than a relationship :) We'll see were things go.

62270945
>>62262891

OnlyFans is always hiring: no experience necessary, work from home at your own hours, no dress code required

62271089
>>62259968
so dont marry. i literally do not understand why you would if you have money and a house?
horny? hire a prostitute.
lonely? get friends, or see above.
existential crisis? realize its fake and gay, we all go into the ground eventually. dead and rotting in the ground.

62271100
>>62270366
i thought the chinese have a huge surplus of men
my mom got pregnant when she was 40 and had me. i only have a touch of 'tism

62271108
>>62268693
All the FIREs are also all bogles. They do not have the brains or cojones to try to get a +3%/year outperformance on the SPY (which I get easily in one of my bad but green months). They do not trust themselves that grand feat.

62271141
>>62263178
Does your "t" button not work?

62271142
>>62261104
Tom Brady is a very swell guy. Everyone should check out his book "The TB12 Method"

62271171
>>62269901
I'm 27, any tips and tricks?

62271254
>>62260803
cant I just do this with crypto and give my mommey the sneed phrase?

item.title: why does this keep happening — 149 replies — 53.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 149 • Updated: 2026-05-25 11:04 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62269820
>>62269792
but nobody cares about fairness for the unwashed masses. what happens in the real world:
>here is this new system
>oh ok, i guess i'll have to deal with that now, like all the other things that are out of my control
>i can buy something like sock in the system? ok cool, i also don't buy co2 certificates, but nice having the option i guess

62269824
>>62269820
You are not ready for the amount of seethe that is to come when LINK is around Google's marketcap.

62269828
>>62269824
can't be worse than the current linkie seethe, i'll manage

62269848
>>62269433
A spiritual war between good and evil.
Currently looking for an alternative to this situation.
Evil collapses through self sabotage on its own because it depends on light like a parasite.
The solution was to do nothing and ignore them.
But then why is it like this?
Are they really that stupid?

62269920
>>62263230
I dumped LINK already for BTC, I held on for years and got nothing but dump. Now I'm all in on BTC and I staked some now that native BTC staking is a thing and I might lend through TBV when its ready.

62270087
>>62268743
Exactly. This anon gets it. Link baggies are literal gme retards schizoing out about hedge fund "short ladder attacks" suppressing them. It seems that every bagholder tends to want to believe in the narrative that """they""" are messing with your bags.

62270949
We'll go through this one by one

>>62269022
Pull up the btc chart on 3-1-21 it was a massive green candle day.

>>62269027
Feb 17 22 on btc you're mid bear market. Btc had large dumps before and after this announcement. Also an opinion piece is not news.

>>62269034
Jun 20 21 Your swift news broke during the middle of the mid cycle dump. Violent dumps were happening constantly in this time.

>>62269035
Apr 17 21 That was the market top. Every faggot and their mother was buying crypto dog coins and nft's.

>>62269039
Aug 24 21 In your own chart btc was already dumping prior to the talk and a speach outside of somebody important is not really news.

>>62269044
checked
Another "why does my token have a resistance level" schizo chart

62270956
It's because link is built for the BTC

62270958
BTC won. tiny linkboi peckers don't cut it.

62270979
>>62269045
Even btc was just crabbing here. It proves nothing.

>>62269049
May 10 20
see >>62268743 there were other tokens doing this same pattern but you only care about and watch link.
May 20 20
Same here
May 21 20
BTC and link are both just crabbing and dumping here. No relevance.
May 24 20
Btc goes down link goes down. No relevance.
May 29 20
Lets zoom out btc and zoom in link so it looks like that pump btc also did doesn't register as well.

>>62269051
1) idk here
2) Alts mid alt season were gaining sats, holy shit amazing take
3) Classic stop hunt. No relevance.
4.1) 4.2) This feels like you're grasping here

>>62269054
Jun 2 20
On the link chart this looks weird, but the BTC chart is literally just chopping around well before the link news.

>>62269057
Oct 10 21
>Alts did better than btc mid alt season
Even still BTC was a doji candle this day and ranup heavily after

>>62269059
Another "why resistance on my token chart"

62270997
>>62270949
>>62270979
It does not matter if Bitcoin is in a bear, a crab, or a bull; the second Link does something Bitcoin nosedives.
Your efforts here only prove that.

Literally how are you this retarded.

62271020
>>62269063
Oct 21 21
>Why did btc just drop during the peak of the wack off distribution pattern which was also mid alt season, don't they know alts were all pumping

>>62269065
Nov 15 21
You mean the pre-anticipatory run-up for the conference was sold off after the conference........WHAT???????

>>62269070
Apr 17 21
We discussed this day already.
Sep 13 21
Cherry pick

>>62269132
>Bitcoin crashed the entire market
As it does, and it will continue until the market actually matures. And even after certain cryptos will be lumped together into industries. Why does Apple's stock go down when bad news happens for Microsoft? Because they are both tech stocks, and people's minds think that what is bad for one must be bad for the other, even if they technically aren't.

>>62269280
>nobody gives a shit about twitter posts
And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters. Everyone just sits in their own comfy place with only the ideas they like. 4 chan is meant to be that open place but it's fill with chuds and incels ( like yourselves) that repeat talking points en masse and don't actually listen to differing input.

62271032
>future of finance
Mais non, mon ami
Is ze future of France, honhonhon
Ker-BOOOOOOOOOMBE
HONHONHONHON

62271040
>>62269410
I think it happens because anons want the idea of "they are out to get you" to be true, and you look for anything to rationalize this to convince yourselves.

>>62269433
>Why they are doing this?
Because so much about random 4chan posters so they devote all their time to create crazy market dumps the exact millisecond anytime they feel joy about their investments which is why your life suck because they have it out for you and not because you spend all day jerking off and posting 4chan....This actually does happen to some degree but it's algorithms farming you

>>62269448
>the people with enough money to suppress entire markets and start/stop wars among countries need more money to gain the power they already have
yes of course

>>62269628
What does making it look like for you?

>>62269680
Sadly probably allot lower than we all want it to be depending is cll releases their financials and implements staking fully and even then it won't be the singularity anons are after.

>>62269681
>they are constantly monitoring /biz/
not the tranime posters cry face

>>62269710
Cll has actively hinted at a not well divulged hiding strategy, which, ergo, would reveal the strategy, and nobody knows what they do with the token or the company in its entirety.

62271062
>>62270997
Yeah duuuuuude it makes perfect sense, whether we are in a bull, bear, or crab market BTC just seems to always giga dump on either link news or pumps, but this is totally normal and coincidental bro duuuuuuuude.
DUUUUUUUUUDE it's not like BTC dumps on LINK news hehe it's just that BTC randomly dumps and takes LINK down with it hehe take your meds dude

62271066
>>62269780
>The unwashed masses who will ask
How many of them have an iphone or android but don't buy apple stock or microsoft? Now tell people to invest into the companies that make software for those devices. It's a moot point to think they're fudding the masses from buying the services that prediction markets or draft kings will use. Link won't matter to these people.

>>62269789
this

>>62269848
The light and dark both exist inside you. There is no inherently evil or good on this earth. Get 10 layers into this kind of thinking and you'll start to see how great God really is to be aware and manage all this.

>>62270997
Link anons have a persecution bias, and won't show you the times their "theories" don't apply.

For example all the charts I responded too are pretty close in the date range. Being all last cycle. Why none from the 2017 era? Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range or even the most recent link news? It's from one anon making the chart and the echo chamber sharing it back and forth for years. It wasn't true to begin with, you've just seen it so many times and look at the backlash I'm getting for even saying I disagree with their narrative. You're not arguing with logic points but the anons own ego.

62271074
>>62271066
>Link anons have a persecution bias
That's pretty normal after like 6 years of outright persecution.

>Why none from the 2017 era?
Here's one from 2018

62271088
>>62271066
>It's a moot point to think they're fudding the masses from buying the services that prediction markets or draft kings will use.
Why do you even answer to me if you don't even understand what I'm saying?
I never said anything about 'fudding' the masses (that's a freudian slip on your part).
In fact they do the opposite, they openly spoonfeed with all the marketing that CLL and Swift are doing so that anyone can buy in now while they suppress and distribute the tokens.
You are so fucking new here buddy and it shows. If you were here since day 1 you would understand that Chainlink was always supposed to be le fair crypto-native network that banks just seemed to adopt because le pristine track record and so on and so forth.
This is of course complete BS because Chainlink IS a Swift project so it's king made, but it could not seem like that for DeFi adoption to look decentralized and for their future plans which you don't seem to grasp at all.
Don't worry, I know that you are just the verbose ripple shill so you have a very sallow understanding of Chainlink..
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHA TAKE A LOAD OF THIS GUY

62271096
>>62271088
>>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
The ENTIRE 2022-2023 (((range))) was ENTIRELY due to BTC dump-based LINK suppression keeping the price in that range.

62271099
>>62271074
Not the gotcha you believe this is. Another lesson from trading stocks. The same people tend to trade and hold the same stocks over and over. What this means is every chart has a temperament to it. Like how there are cats, but not all cats are the same. Got it? Different temperament. So the link chart, from what you all continually show, is that links temperament is for consistent flat resistance levels. This probably won't change for the link until enough new investors/traders join the morphus blob of temperament to change it. Which is good because it lends some credibility to link repeating the same patterns so you can profit from them.

62271104
>>62271099
>Why none from the 2017 era?
>Gets shown one from the 2017 era
>Instant wall of pilpul
hahahaha

62271106
>>62271096
lel I was just about to post this.
This faggot is as blind and clueless as he is wordy and spammy.

And now he's going all new age on us:
>>62271099
>What this means is every chart has a temperament to it.

Dude's about to break out the mood crystals.

62271116
>>62271020
>And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters
you clearly do, which is why you are here calling people "schizos" for noticing BTC dumps whenever LINK gets positive news (pic rel). i dont spend a single minute discussing people in xrp threads or twitter: why would you post 26 times in this thread?

you have done this before havent you

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

ETH is fucking doomed isn't it? All alts are fucked. Crypto is fucking screwed.

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-25 10:57 UTC

OP

ETH is fucking doomed isn't it? All alts are fucked. Crypto is fucking screwed.

Early replies (first 3)

62270555
>>62270545
yes it sucks

62270558
>>62270545
First bear market, Anon?

62270570
no because when the strait stays closed and oil moons, there will be an oil export ban and a national draft reinstated, which will cause money to flow into any assets the US cant seize from people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62270590
Who fucking knows anymore, stocks are surging every day. No one gives a fuck about incel crypto garbage. ETH should have hit 10k this cycle. It barely broke ath. Adjusting for inflation, it actually didn't.

62270594
>>62270545
hbar and xrp chads win?

62270600
>>62270545
no, you can still use ETH to uuhhhh
and when Iran bullshit ends, everyone will buy ETH to uhhhhh so that they can uhhhhh
if we all pretend that there's no paypal and zelle then it's basically the future of finance

62270603
It feels like no one is speculating on crypto anymore, its just us retards copeing in denial, mentally unable to "read the room" because we honestly have no back up plan if crypto fails so we're forced to stay here and hope it eventually gets back the popularity it had in 2017 and 2021

62270616
looks like vitalik and the other co-founders are jumping ship and leaving future development up to Tom Lee lol

>That said, there are aspects of supporting ETH the asset - *necessary* aspects even - that are outside the scope of the EF. This is where we need other heroes (some of whom hold more ETH than the EF does) to step in and help. EF has been recently thinking more about how it will relate to other such organizations, and give them needed initial support.

62270620
>>62270616
>(some of whom hold more ETH than the EF does)
the audacity of these niggers, they dumped nonstop for years and now they say this

62270622
>>62270545
All crypto is doomed. You have been fooled by meaningless technobabble chanted at your weak mind, convincing you that you're smart and way ahead of the rest of the world, when you're just a midwit gambler. It's all garbage and you're going to lose everything.

As it gets worse and worse, the technobabble will get crazier.

62270985
>>62270620
This! I am a core ethnigger and i ll never sell, but behead every ef niggers!

62270986
>>62270985
Fukken leeches everywhere in this shithole "space"....

62270987
>>62270622
technobabble has actually decreased and the space has become more retarded.

62270992
>>62270622
Reads like a /biz/ post from 2015, lol

t. oldfag

62270999
>>62270545
What does it do?
Besides being a copy of bitcoin
Meme coins have stained the image of alts forever, while bitcoin has attained actual asset status

62271003
>>62270992
Ofc, its just a default bear, niggers are stupid here and eth is ded according to them at 2100 pesos, but still fuck vb for l2 bs and ef for. Non stop dumping and not living from staking rewards, they deserve an oldschool. Beheading.

62271014
>>62270999
It scaled mainnet, much faster than btc, much cheaper, and it is programmable money unlike lil collectibles le 21m btc what you can wqtch in your wallet doing nothing.

62271233
>>62270999
There are some really neat ideas in Eth atm;

If people understood how liquidity pools actually worked, that alone would probably change the world.
There are two major dominos that will let Eth hit 10k-20k.

Domino #1: Stablecoin being integrated into payment rails. Metamask is coming out with a credit card, that spends stablecoins instead of real currency. This will basically mean that normies can spend tokenized currency like real currency, in some cases without even knowing they're doing a blockchain transaction.

Domino #2: Companies issuing tokenized assets for goods and services. Ie; Tesla selling a car by selling a 'Token', that can be redeemed at any Tesla outlet, for a car. But can also be traded freely, split, put into liquidity pools, used as collateral, pump and dumped etc.

Once these two dominos fall, Crypto will dwarf AI again as the hot new thing, and most of it will happen on Ethereum and layer2 Eth chains.

62271257
>>62271233
I had wet dreams too :(

62271289
>>62270545
>Diversify
Much like fiat currencies often fall crypto will likely have similar 'cycles'. Ultimately crypto is just another means of accelerating the rate you gather more fundamental resources, which will help you have an easier time if such a 'fall' happens in your lifetime.

62271394
I don't even know why is ETH still relevant other than for being used for gas to pay for Lending protocols/stables and such.
The shitcoin casino is dead since everybody moved to Solana, and no normie nor a boomer believes in Alts and everybody knows altseason will never be back.
ETH price should be $700 being generous

item.title: Bitcoin Rainbow Chart Says BUY — 11 replies — 5.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin Rainbow Chart Says BUY

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-25 11:52 UTC

OP

This is a once in a lifetime opportunity to buy BTC, STRC, MSTR, ASST, SATA, etc. and earn life changing money.

These next 1-2 years will be huge for Bitcoin.
Everything is about to launch like a rocket ship.
200 day moving average is about to cross at low 80k, all the BTC is being bought up supply squeeze, CLARITY act, banks, institutions, countries, it's the golden bull.

Early replies (first 3)

62270355
>>62270347
So fxcking bullish btc

62270386
>>62270355
Not even joking how do they stop this?

The Rainbow.

62270447
>>62270386
By not buying. Nobody stops you from being a sucker in a ponzi market with no demand

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62270493
>>62270347
SATA and STRC don't scale with Bitcoin though. They're just for free dividend income.

62271070
>>62270347
7k shares of ASST here. $15 avg. Gonna be a sweet sweet ride my friend.

62271073
>>62270493
yup, if u want exponential growth, u ditch sata or strc. They are just financial vehicles to add fuel to the fire.

62271084
The rainbow is DYING

62271223
>>62270493
STRC and SATA are tax deferred dividends.

You earn like 11-13% interest in dividends and instead of paying tax your cost basis just keeps going down, and you pay long term cap gains at the end.
So buy at $100 and that number goes to 99, 98, 95, etc. cost basis as you collect sweet tax free dividends.

MSTR and ASST will probably run hard, but ultimately idk what they'll do with diluting shares and smash buying the top again, lol.

62271438
>>62270347

62271473
>>62270347
What's your price predictions OP

>>62265351
yeah except he is some virgin ass loser giving the ick to everything with a vagina so he will have to pay double

62265451
>>62265215
You know poop comes outta that thing, right?

62265697
>>62265442
nah some things can't be bought at all

62266079
>>62265451
Yeah and that's kinda hot
>>62265442
True, I don't mind paying double for such a rarified experience.
>>62265697
I hope not
>>62265351
I consider OP webmrel woman hot and not dumpy. I like her glasses and they make me diamonds.

62266371
This is how you get pinkeye and invited to the special Link discord.

62266460
>>62265132
Her name is literally coal

62266464
>>62266371
worth it

62268252
>>62265132
Is that really Vitalik? Or just some other skinny geek?

62268289
>>62265132
Over 15% of my $2 mil net worth is in ETH. The entire premise of me making it for the rest of my life rests on ETH getting to $20k+. Hell no I'm not selling now.

62268290
>>62268289
>Over 15% of my $2 mil net worth is in ETH
wheres the rest ?

62268297
>>62268290
20% bitcoin, 10% home equity, and 70% in stocks

62268541
>>62265215
what is the benefit?
does he get a boner from this?
then what let it go?

62268562
>>62268252
It's him kek. This was literally the day ETH started its drawdown too.

62268570
im waiting for 2.2k to dump my stack

62268839
>>62268541
For me, the benefit is to be able to touch, be smothered by, and worship an attractive woman's ass.
t. khhv

62268843
>>62268562
Thanks. I knew he was a weirdo but yuck.

>>62265215
Well, Vitalik made over a billion dollars off ETH bagholders, so. . . .

62268848
>>62268839
Only 5 feloony's? You've got to pump those numbers up!

62268854
>>62268848
I have no felonies anon

62268856
>>62268848
>>62268854
Oh I see you're referring to my ID. Yeah, sometimes I get an odd one.

62269819
>>62265215
>humiliation ritual
ESLs really are so retarded. You don't know what that means

62269825
>>62269819
I'm EOP not ESL, and I was using le meme phrase

62269861
>>62265215
ETH fanboy following in his master's footsteps. Pathetic!

62269866
>>62269861
I have 0 (zero) ETH. I've never had it.

62269892
>>62265137
I sold and bought BTC, staked it, see you 2030.

62269902
>>62268843
>>62268562
>>62268252
it's obviously not vitalik you retard.

62270988
>>62265132
Last summer, you fucktard.

62271024
>>62266371
